headline: Gareth Southgate
body: England’s camaraderie built under Gareth Southgate and been enhanced by Thomas Tuchel. Club England, Band of Brothers feel embodied in the dressing-room comedy double act of John Stones and Declan Rice, Manchester City and Arsenal, pranking Tuchel. “We go hard at it for 38 games of the season and those two they're at it all season for the league and then to come here, you completely forget about it,” says their England team-mate, Aston Villa’s Morgan Rogers. As he spoke in the England media bunker after training, Rogers was interrupted by Tottenham Hotspur’s Djed Spence with an urgent question: “Been a lot of speculation over who’s the fastest in the squad,” asked Spence. Rogers replied: “Let's just say I wouldn't bet against you in a race! But there's a couple saying they might have you. Anthony Gordon being one of them. A snapshot of the togetherness. Club cliques no more. Rogers: “You almost forget what clubs everyone plays for. It’s just everyone together as one. You can see that coming to show in games and in big moments, and then in the celebrations after with those two being silly!” The pranking – Stones pretending he’d injured his shoulder – came as England celebrated overcoming adversity in the Azteca on Sunday, going down to 10 men, and holding on to beat Mexico. Rogers: “Everyone can have good players and win games off ability, but we knew we had that difference of togetherness, grit, determination. “We showed the world we can win in different ways with different atmospheres, different environments. That gives the fans back home that extra belief that no matter how the game's going we've got the ability to find a way.” Rogers loves those special moments after the final whistle, standing in front of the fans, singing “Wonderwall”, sharing the moment and the joy of victory. “You're in awe of how many fans there are, how much it means and how passionate they are. It means everything to us.” Rogers is being heavily linked with a move to Arsenal. He’s too bright and respectful to be drawn on the subject. The 23-year-old talked, instead, of his desire to keep improving, to be more consistent, inspired by the high achievers around him with England. “I look at players in the room, in the training ground, the Harry Kanes, Judes. They've done it for five, six, seven, eight years consistently. That's the difference from a good player to a great player. I want to take that step. I want to be in those conversations as the best player in England and Europe. I’ve still got a long way to go and a lot of learning to do but I think I'm on the right path of learning from these players. I'm getting my consistency right.” Rogers also learns about consistency of performance from Ezri Konsa, a mentor at Villa. “He's a massive leader, a massive character here and at Villa. He's helped me in difficult moments this season, last season, just that quiet, calming word. He's never too high, never too low. He's just consistent, so he's a good person to lean on for me. “He's one of the best centre-backs at this World Cup. He never gets beaten, strong in the air, strong in the tackle, unbelievable on the ball. I honestly don't know a flaw in his game.” He’s under-rated. “That's helped Villa as probably a bigger club has taken him in the last few years. It's nice that he gets the recognition and he can compete on the world stage and everyone can how good he actually is. He does go under the radar massively.” With players of the calibre of Kane, Bellingham, and Konsa, and Rogers coming off the bench, the Villa attacker admits they are dreaming of winning the World Cup. “We're human and you can't help think that every game won is a step closer to the end goal - but if you take your eye off the ball for any second that's where you can get punished.” ENG AVFC FifaWorldCup Video
raw_text:
England’s camaraderie built under Gareth Southgate and been enhanced by Thomas Tuchel. Club England, Band of Brothers feel embodied in the dressing-room comedy double act of John Stones and Declan Rice, Manchester City and Arsenal, pranking Tuchel. “We go hard at it for 38 games of the season and those two they're at it all season for the league and then to come here, you completely forget about it,” says their England team-mate, Aston Villa’s Morgan Rogers. 

As he spoke in the England media bunker after training, Rogers was interrupted by Tottenham Hotspur’s Djed Spence with an urgent question: “Been a lot of speculation over who’s the fastest in the squad,” asked Spence. Rogers replied: “Let's just say I wouldn't bet against you in a race! But there's a couple saying they might have you. Anthony Gordon being one of them."

A snapshot of the togetherness. Club cliques no more.  Rogers: “You almost forget what clubs everyone plays for. It’s just everyone together as one. You can see that coming to show in games and in big moments, and then in the celebrations after with those two being silly!”

The pranking – Stones pretending he’d injured his shoulder – came as England celebrated overcoming adversity in the Azteca on Sunday, going down to 10 men, and holding on to beat Mexico. Rogers: “Everyone can have good players and win games off ability, but we knew we had that difference of togetherness, grit, determination. 

 “We showed the world we can win in different ways with different atmospheres, different environments. That gives the fans back home that extra belief that no matter how the game's going we've got the ability to find a way.” Rogers loves those special moments after the final whistle, standing in front of the fans, singing “Wonderwall”, sharing the moment and the joy of victory. “You're in awe of how many fans there are, how much it means and how passionate they are. It means everything to us.”

Rogers is being heavily linked with a move to Arsenal. He’s too bright and respectful to be drawn on the subject. The 23-year-old talked, instead, of his desire to keep improving, to be more consistent, inspired by the high achievers around him with England. “I look at players in the room, in the training ground, the Harry Kanes, Judes. They've done it for five, six, seven, eight years consistently. 

"That's the difference from a good player to a great player. I want to take that step. I want to be in those conversations as the best player in England and Europe. I’ve still got a long way to go and a lot of learning to do but I think I'm on the right path of learning from these players. I'm getting my consistency right.”

Rogers also learns about consistency of performance from Ezri Konsa, a mentor at Villa. “He's a massive leader, a massive character here and at Villa. He's helped me in difficult moments this season, last season, just that quiet, calming word. He's never too high, never too low. He's just consistent, so he's a good person to lean on for me.

“He's one of the best centre-backs at this World Cup. He never gets beaten, strong in the air, strong in the tackle, unbelievable on the ball. I honestly don't know a flaw in his game.” He’s under-rated. “That's helped Villa as probably a bigger club has taken him in the last few years. It's nice that he gets the recognition and he can compete on the world stage and everyone can how good he actually is. He does go under the radar massively.”

With players of the calibre of Kane, Bellingham, and Konsa, and Rogers coming off the bench, the Villa attacker admits they are dreaming of winning the World Cup. “We're human and you can't help think that every game won is a step closer to the end goal - but if you take your eye off the ball for any second that's where you can get punished.” #ENG #AVFC #FifaWorldCup

Video

It's nice that he gets the recognition and he can compete on the world stage and everyone can how good he actually is. He does go under the radar massively.”

With players of the calibre of Kane, Bellingham, and Konsa, and Rogers coming off the bench, the Villa attacker admits they are dreaming of winning the World Cup. “We're human and you can't help think that every game won is a step closer to the end goal - but if you take your eye off the ball for any second that's where you can get punished.” #ENG #AVFC #FifaWorldCup

draft_caption:
🚑 INJURY- GARETH SOUTHGATE (MANCHESTER CITY) BEING ASSESSED.
⏱️ RETURN — TBC

#MCFC #Arsenal #InjuryNews #PremierLeague
